Now I understand the full picture. The `changes` property can be:
1. `undefined` - no changes tracked
2. An array of `IChatSessionFileChange[]` - detailed changes
3. An object with `{files: number, insertions: number, deletions: number}` - summary

The bug is in the logic at lines 164-174. When a session is finished and has a `changes` object (even if all counts are 0), it enters the first if block but doesn't render anything if there are no actual changes. It should fall back to rendering the d
